message:
test(conformance): broaden fprintf arg-shape coverage in run_impl + run_host

Extends the run_impl_fprintf / run_host_fprintf dispatch match in
crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs so the conformance harness
can exercise a wider slice of real-world fprintf call shapes against
both frankenlibc and the host libc side-by-side, instead of bailing
with "unsupported fprintf arg combination" for common format strings
that mix integers, strings, and doubles.

New PrintfArg slice patterns, mirrored between run_impl_fprintf and
run_host_fprintf (so the two sides stay symmetric -- any shape
accepted by one must be accepted by the other):

- [NullPtr] -- passes a literal `std::ptr::null::<c_char>()` so we can
  exercise `%s` with a NULL argument and compare whether the
  frankenlibc stdio layer produces the same "(null)" / unsupported
  outcome as the host glibc version.
- [Int, Int, Double] -- classic "%d %d %f" triple used by numeric
  formatting conformance cases.
- [Int, Int, Int] -- "%d %d %d" triple (timestamps, 3-tuples).
- [Int, Int, Str] -- "%d: %d (%s)" style pattern where the string
  argument trails the ints.
- [Int, Str, Double] -- "#%d %s = %f" pattern.
- [Int, Int, Str, Double] -- 4-arg shape such as
  "%d/%d: %s = %f".
- [Int, Int, Int, Int, Int] -- 5-int tuple such as
  "%d.%d.%d.%d.%d" (versions, IPv4 octets, debug counters).

Each new arm forwards the tuple straight to
`frankenlibc_abi::stdio_abi::fprintf` (Rust side) or `libc::fprintf`
(host side) inside the existing `unsafe` block, passing `*a0, *a1,
...` for integers/doubles and `a.as_ptr()` for `PrintfArg::Str`,
exactly matching the two-argument arms already above. No change to
the fallback `_ => Err("unsupported fprintf arg combination")`
behavior -- just more shapes land in the fast path before we fall
through to the error.

No production ABI change; this is pure conformance-harness surface
expansion. It lets the existing fprintf fixture tables pick up
format strings that were previously being silently skipped /
erroring, and it keeps the host-vs-impl paths strictly symmetric so
differential comparisons stay valid.

files.patch:
@@ -2910,6 +2910,40 @@ fn run_impl_fprintf(
         [PrintfArg::Str(a0), PrintfArg::Str(a1)] => unsafe {
             frankenlibc_abi::stdio_abi::fprintf(stream, fmt, a0.as_ptr(), a1.as_ptr())
         },
+        [PrintfArg::NullPtr] => unsafe {
+            frankenlibc_abi::stdio_abi::fprintf(stream, fmt, std::ptr::null::<c_char>())
+        },
+        [
+            PrintfArg::Int(a0),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a1),
+            PrintfArg::Double(a2),
+        ] => unsafe { frankenlibc_abi::stdio_abi::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, *a1, *a2) },
+        [PrintfArg::Int(a0), PrintfArg::Int(a1), PrintfArg::Int(a2)] => unsafe {
+            frankenlibc_abi::stdio_abi::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, *a1, *a2)
+        },
+        [PrintfArg::Int(a0), PrintfArg::Int(a1), PrintfArg::Str(a2)] => unsafe {
+            frankenlibc_abi::stdio_abi::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, *a1, a2.as_ptr())
+        },
+        [
+            PrintfArg::Int(a0),
+            PrintfArg::Str(a1),
+            PrintfArg::Double(a2),
+        ] => unsafe { frankenlibc_abi::stdio_abi::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, a1.as_ptr(), *a2) },
+        [
+            PrintfArg::Int(a0),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a1),
+            PrintfArg::Str(a2),
+            PrintfArg::Double(a3),
+        ] => unsafe {
+            frankenlibc_abi::stdio_abi::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, *a1, a2.as_ptr(), *a3)
+        },
+        [
+            PrintfArg::Int(a0),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a1),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a2),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a3),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a4),
+        ] => unsafe { frankenlibc_abi::stdio_abi::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, *a1, *a2, *a3, *a4) },
         _ => {
             return Err(format!("unsupported fprintf arg combination: {:?}", args));
         }
@@ -2939,6 +2973,36 @@ fn run_host_fprintf(
         [PrintfArg::Str(a0), PrintfArg::Str(a1)] => unsafe {
             libc::fprintf(stream, fmt, a0.as_ptr(), a1.as_ptr())
         },
+        [PrintfArg::NullPtr] => unsafe { libc::fprintf(stream, fmt, std::ptr::null::<c_char>()) },
+        [
+            PrintfArg::Int(a0),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a1),
+            PrintfArg::Double(a2),
+        ] => unsafe { libc::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, *a1, *a2) },
+        [PrintfArg::Int(a0), PrintfArg::Int(a1), PrintfArg::Int(a2)] => unsafe {
+            libc::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, *a1, *a2)
+        },
+        [PrintfArg::Int(a0), PrintfArg::Int(a1), PrintfArg::Str(a2)] => unsafe {
+            libc::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, *a1, a2.as_ptr())
+        },
+        [
+            PrintfArg::Int(a0),
+            PrintfArg::Str(a1),
+            PrintfArg::Double(a2),
+        ] => unsafe { libc::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, a1.as_ptr(), *a2) },
+        [
+            PrintfArg::Int(a0),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a1),
+            PrintfArg::Str(a2),
+            PrintfArg::Double(a3),
+        ] => unsafe { libc::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, *a1, a2.as_ptr(), *a3) },
+        [
+            PrintfArg::Int(a0),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a1),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a2),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a3),
+            PrintfArg::Int(a4),
+        ] => unsafe { libc::fprintf(stream, fmt, *a0, *a1, *a2, *a3, *a4) },
         _ => {
             return Err(format!("unsupported fprintf arg combination: {:?}", args));
         }
